mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, directly increasing present valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in the unemployment rate above a 0.2 percentage point threshold over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or t-test p-value exceeds 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising real sales of autos and falling housing starts indicates a structural shift in consumer demand from durable goods to real estate, creating a positive momentum signal for S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Interaction term loses statistical significance with p-value above 0.10 in out-of-sample regression over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth above its 12-month moving average signals strong supply-side momentum, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days specifically during periods of low inflation.
failure_condition: Signal direction flips to negative during low inflation periods or Rank IC drops below 0.02 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect robust economic activity and corporate earnings growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation print exceeding 0.3% triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days due to higher discount rates.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of triggered instances, or signal loses predictive power during high inflation regimes.

mechanism_narrative: High real GDP growth combined with low federal funds rates creates an expansionary macro environment that sustains equity valuations, predicting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint effect becomes insignificant or reverses sign in out-of-sample testing over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in real auto sales exceeding 2% signals weakening consumer demand, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as earnings expectations are downgraded.
failure_condition: Positive returns occur in > 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC falls below 0.03 during late-cycle phases.
